methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Kraken Robotics is a publicly listed company (TSX-V) operating in the Defense & Security sector. The peer universe for this company is constrained: the sector contains 21 total peers, but only 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI are identified. This fragmentation limits the applicability of conventional multiples-based valuation approaches.

The peer-median method is not applicable in this analysis. A filter for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparables yielded fewer than three datapoints in each category—the minimum threshold required for robust median estimation. Applying an all-sector median across the full 21-peer Defense & Security universe would introduce substantial measurement error given the heterogeneity in business models, technology maturity, and addressable markets within the sector.

Without sufficient narrow peer clustering and absent triangulated valuation outputs in the provided data, no specific range can be derived from the methods attempted. Key uncertainties limiting this analysis include the small public comp set (n=2), the difficulty isolating true stage-matched private peers, and the absence of transaction or financial multiple data. Further valuation work would require either expanded peer identification within narrower technology or end-market subsegments, or reliance on alternative methods such as discounted cash flow or venture-backed precedent transactions.
